Normalisation

Published

2026-07-23

Normalisation (3)

Protocol reader guidance

This notebook is written for users applying the workflow to their own dataset. Read each section in order, confirm required inputs before running code sections, and use the YAML parameters at the top of the file to adapt paths and thresholds for your study design.

Before you run

Validate input data and metadata column names first. Most runtime issues in protocol use are caused by path mismatches, missing files, or dataset-specific column naming differences.

Path portability check

The setup section checks the full input and output path lengths against portable_path_limit before analysis begins. Generated diagnostic filenames are shortened in a fixed, repeatable way when needed, and the fixed filenames that later stages must read are never renamed. Fix any path flagged as too long before continuing.

Reproducibility caution

When editing parameters, record exactly what changed (paths, thresholds, model options) in your run notes so later figures and conclusions can be reproduced during review and publication.

This notebook runs the normalisation stage for GeoMx data using the QC-filtered object as the required input. GeoDiff outputs are optional: if a GeoDiff-filtered count object or existing GeoDiff-normalized output is present, the GeoDiff option is used; if not, GeoDiff-only sections are skipped and Q3/TMM normalisation continues.

It computes multiple normalisation representations (Trimmed Mean of M-values (TMM), Q3, negative-probe, and, when available, GeoDiff-normalized normmat), saves processed objects, and generates comparison plots to support later method selection.

The focus here is to document what is executed and why; this document does not interpret the resulting plots or metrics.

What each saved assay contains

This stage preserves raw count values in exprs for GeoMx objects. Q3-normalized values are written to the assay element configured by params$q3_toElt (q_norm by default), negative-probe-normalized values are written to params$neg_toElt, and GeoDiff-normalized values are written to normmat. The GeoDiff-normalized normmat assay is created from the Stage 2 GeoDiff-filtered count object (GeoDiff_spatial_data.RDS). The TMM output is an edgeR DGEList that stores raw counts plus TMM scaling factors rather than replacing the count matrix with normalized expression values.

How to interpret this stage

Use this stage to make explicit, reproducible decisions before progressing:

  1. Confirm the key output objects/tables were produced without unexpected warnings.
  2. Review the stage figures in the context of your study design (technical quality, group balance, and model assumptions).
  3. Record your decision in run notes (for example: “acceptable”, “rerun with adjusted parameters”, or “exclude affected samples/groups”) before moving to the next notebook.
Decision checkpoint

For publication-grade protocol reporting, capture what you observed, what decision you made, and which parameter settings were used at this stage.

Load libraries

Load package dependencies for normalisation workflows, plotting, and data IO.

Show code 
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(NanoStringNCTools)
  library(GeomxTools)
  library(GeoMxWorkflows)
  library(testthat)
  library(tidyverse)
  library(cowplot)
  library(janitor)
  library(edgeR)
  library(RUVSeq)
  library(DESeq2)
  library(limma)
  library(matrixStats)
  library(standR)
  library(fs)
  library(here)
  library(glue)
})

protocol_html_family <- "Segoe UI"
protocol_plot_family <- "sans"

if (.Platform$OS.type == "windows") {
  grDevices::windowsFonts(sans = grDevices::windowsFont(protocol_html_family))
}

protocol_cowplot_theme <- function(...) {
  cowplot::theme_cowplot(font_family = protocol_plot_family, ...)
}
protocol_bw_theme <- function(...) {
  ggplot2::theme_bw(base_family = protocol_plot_family, ...)
}
normalisation_boxplot_axis_text <- ggplot2::element_text(
  family = protocol_plot_family,
  angle = 90,
  vjust = 0.5,
  hjust = 1,
  size = 4
)
protocol_rotated_axis_text <- ggplot2::element_text(
  family = protocol_plot_family,
  angle = 90,
  vjust = 0.5,
  hjust = 1
)

ggplot2::theme_set(protocol_cowplot_theme())

Setup paths

Resolve input/output paths from YAML params and create output folders for this stage.

Show code 
root <- params$project_root
# Auto-detect root when YAML root is blank/placeholder during interactive runs.
if (is.null(root) || root == "" || root == ".") {
  root <- here::here()
} else {
  root <- path_abs(root)
}
# here()-first helper with explicit YAML-root override when provided.
root_via_here <- path_abs(here::here())
use_here_root <- identical(path_abs(root), root_via_here)
project_path <- function(...) {
  if (use_here_root) {
    here::here(...)
  } else {
    path(root, ...)
  }
}

# Shared source helpers
dir_src <- project_path(params$dir_src)
path_utilities_path <- path(dir_src, "pathUtilities.R")
if (!file_exists(path_utilities_path)) {
  stop("Required path helper not found: ", path_utilities_path, call. = FALSE)
}
source(path_utilities_path)
portable_path_limit <- protocol_validate_path_limit(params$portable_path_limit)
utility_functions_path <- path(dir_src, "utilityFunctions.R")
if (!file_exists(utility_functions_path)) {
  stop(
    "Required helper file not found: ", utility_functions_path,
    ". Set params$project_root to the project root and params$dir_src to the helper directory name, usually 'src'.",
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

# Input paths
qc_filtered_path <- project_path(params$dir_outputs, params$qc_stage, params$qc_results_dir, params$qc_filtered_rds)
geodiff_path     <- project_path(params$dir_outputs, params$geodiff_stage, params$geodiff_results_dir, params$geodiff_rds)

# Output paths
out_base    <- project_path(params$dir_outputs, params$out_stage)
out_results <- path(out_base, params$out_results)
out_tables  <- path(out_base, params$out_tables)
out_figures <- path(out_base, params$out_figures)
out_fig_norm <- path(out_figures, params$fig_subdir)

# Expected persisted objects for optional reload mode.
tmm_rds_path <- protocol_contract_path(out_results, "TMM_normalised_spatial_data.RDS", portable_path_limit, "Stage 3 TMM object")
tmm_minimal_counts_rds_path <- protocol_contract_path(out_results, "TMM_normalised_spatial_minimal_Counts_data.RDS", portable_path_limit, "Stage 3 minimal TMM object")
normalised_rds_path <- protocol_contract_path(out_results, "normalised_spatial_data.RDS", portable_path_limit, "Stage 3 normalized object")
geodiff_norm_rds_path <- protocol_contract_path(out_results, "GeoDiff_normalised_spatial_data.RDS", portable_path_limit, "Stage 3 GeoDiff-normalized object")

stage3_path_audit <- protocol_path_audit(
  c(
    "Stage 1 filtered input" = qc_filtered_path,
    "Optional Stage 2 input" = geodiff_path,
    "Q3 normalized object" = normalised_rds_path,
    "TMM normalized object" = tmm_rds_path,
    "GeoDiff-normalized object" = geodiff_norm_rds_path,
    "Normalization figure directory" = out_fig_norm
  ),
  portable_path_limit
)
protocol_assert_audit(stage3_path_audit, portable_path_limit)
knitr::kable(stage3_path_audit, caption = "Stage 3 path-portability audit")
Stage 3 path-portability audit
path_role resolved_path path_characters remaining_characters status
Stage 1 filtered input C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/1_QC/results/filtered_spatial_data.RDS 71 129 Portable
Optional Stage 2 input C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/2_GeoDiff/results/GeoDiff_spatial_data.RDS 75 125 Portable
Q3 normalized object C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/3_Norm/results/normalised_spatial_data.RDS 75 125 Portable
TMM normalized object C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/3_Norm/results/TMM_normalised_spatial_data.RDS 79 121 Portable
GeoDiff-normalized object C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/3_Norm/results/GeoDiff_normalised_spatial_data.RDS 83 117 Portable
Normalization figure directory C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/3_Norm/figures/Normalisation 61 139 Portable
Show code 
dir_create(out_results, recurse = TRUE)
dir_create(out_tables, recurse = TRUE)
# Create figure directory only when plot saving is requested.
if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) dir_create(out_fig_norm, recurse = TRUE)

message(glue::glue("ROOT: {root}"))
message(glue::glue("Shared source helpers: {dir_src}"))
message(glue::glue("QC filtered input: {qc_filtered_path}"))
message(glue::glue("GeoDiff-filtered count object input: {geodiff_path}"))
message(glue::glue("Normalisation output base: {out_base}"))

geodiff_input_exists <- file_exists(geodiff_path)
geodiff_norm_exists <- file_exists(geodiff_norm_rds_path)
geodiff_route_available <- geodiff_input_exists || geodiff_norm_exists
geodiff_route_active <- FALSE
geodiff_skip_message <- glue::glue(
  "GeoDiff optional route skipped: no GeoDiff-filtered count object was found at {geodiff_path} ",
  "and no existing GeoDiff-normalized output was found at {geodiff_norm_rds_path}. ",
  "Q3 and TMM normalisation will continue; GeoDiff-normalized outputs and plots will not be produced."
)

Load filtered inputs and resolve metadata columns

Load the required QC-filtered object and, when available, optional GeoDiff outputs. The Stage 2 GeoDiff input is the GeoDiff-filtered count object; the Stage 3 GeoDiff output is the GeoDiff-normalized normmat object. If GeoDiff files are absent, the notebook reports the missing paths and continues with Q3/TMM normalisation.

Show code 
suppressPackageStartupMessages(
  suppressMessages(
    suppressWarnings(source(utility_functions_path))
  )
)
target_spatial_data  <- readRDS(qc_filtered_path)
spatial_data_geoDiff <- NULL
ROIs_high <- character()

geodiff_status_table <- tibble::tibble(
  Input = c("QC-filtered input", "GeoDiff-filtered count object", "Existing GeoDiff-normalized output"),
  Required_for_stage = c("Yes", "No", "No"),
  Path = c(qc_filtered_path, geodiff_path, geodiff_norm_rds_path),
  File_found = c(file_exists(qc_filtered_path), geodiff_input_exists, geodiff_norm_exists),
  Action = c(
    "Used for Q3, negative-probe, and TMM normalisation.",
    ifelse(geodiff_input_exists, "Will be used to create GeoDiff-normalized normmat.", "GeoDiff normalization will be skipped unless an existing GeoDiff-normalized output is available."),
    ifelse(geodiff_norm_exists, "Can be used for GeoDiff-normalized plots/output reuse.", "No existing GeoDiff-normalized output is available.")
  )
)

knitr::kable(
  geodiff_status_table,
  caption = "Normalisation input file availability. GeoDiff files are optional: when absent, Q3/TMM normalisation continues and GeoDiff-only outputs are skipped."
)
Normalisation input file availability. GeoDiff files are optional: when absent, Q3/TMM normalisation continues and GeoDiff-only outputs are skipped.
Input Required_for_stage Path File_found Action
QC-filtered input Yes C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/1_QC/results/filtered_spatial_data.RDS TRUE Used for Q3, negative-probe, and TMM normalisation.
GeoDiff-filtered count object No C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/2_GeoDiff/results/GeoDiff_spatial_data.RDS TRUE Will be used to create GeoDiff-normalized normmat.
Existing GeoDiff-normalized output No C:/R/gmx/Example_Dataset/Outputs/3_Norm/results/GeoDiff_normalised_spatial_data.RDS FALSE No existing GeoDiff-normalized output is available.
Show code 
if (geodiff_input_exists) {
  spatial_data_geoDiff <- readRDS(geodiff_path)
  message(glue::glue("GeoDiff optional route enabled: GeoDiff-filtered count object found at {geodiff_path}."))
} else if (geodiff_norm_exists) {
  message(glue::glue(
    "GeoDiff optional route enabled from existing output: no GeoDiff-filtered count object was found at {geodiff_path}, ",
    "but an existing GeoDiff-normalized output was found at {geodiff_norm_rds_path}."
  ))
} else {
  message(geodiff_skip_message)
}

# Display metadata inventory with explicit interpretation for protocol readers.
metadata_columns <- colnames(pData(target_spatial_data))
metadata_column_inventory <- tibble::tibble(
  column_name = metadata_columns,
  interpretation = "Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data)."
)

knitr::kable(
  metadata_column_inventory,
  caption = "Metadata column inventory in normalisation input. Use this table to confirm YAML column mappings before plotting."
)
Metadata column inventory in normalisation input. Use this table to confirm YAML column mappings before plotting.
column_name interpretation
lws_source_file Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
lws_source_row Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
library_name Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
slide name Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
segment Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
area Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
location Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
patient_id Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
disease_status Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
cell_type Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
sample_id_raw Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
slide_name Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
NegGeoMean_GSE226829_Hs_R_NGS_WTA_v1.0 Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
NegGeoSD_GSE226829_Hs_R_NGS_WTA_v1.0 Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
LOQ Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
GenesDetected Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
GeneDetectionRate Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
DetectionThreshold Candidate metadata field available in pData(target_spatial_data).
Show code 
# Provide keyword-based helper matches with explicit fallback messaging.
metadata_keyword_matches <- tibble::tibble(
  keyword = c("slide", "annot", "region"),
  matched_columns = c(
    paste(grep("slide", metadata_columns, ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE), collapse = ", "),
    paste(grep("annot", metadata_columns, ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE), collapse = ", "),
    paste(grep("region", metadata_columns, ignore.case = TRUE, value = TRUE), collapse = ", ")
  )
) %>%
  mutate(
    matched_columns = dplyr::if_else(
      matched_columns == "",
      "No columns matched this keyword in the current dataset.",
      matched_columns
    )
  )

knitr::kable(
  metadata_keyword_matches,
  caption = "Keyword-based metadata matches. These are helper hints only; set final column mappings via params$meta_cols."
)
Keyword-based metadata matches. These are helper hints only; set final column mappings via params$meta_cols.
keyword matched_columns
slide slide name, slide_name
annot No columns matched this keyword in the current dataset.
region No columns matched this keyword in the current dataset.
Show code 
# Resolve metadata column names from params

meta_cols <- params$meta_cols
# Unwrap Quarto list params that are nested under `value:`.
if (is.list(meta_cols) && "value" %in% names(meta_cols)) meta_cols <- meta_cols$value

col_slide   <- meta_cols$slide
col_segment <- meta_cols$segment
col_annot   <- meta_cols$annotation
col_region  <- meta_cols$region

# Segment and annotation are required for marker and sample-label plots.
# Slide and disease status add context to distribution plots and are handled gracefully when absent.
metadata_available <- function(col_name, pheno) {
  !is.null(col_name) &&
    length(col_name) == 1 &&
    !is.na(col_name) &&
    nzchar(col_name) &&
    col_name %in% colnames(pheno)
}

target_pheno <- pData(target_spatial_data)
required_meta_cols <- unique(c(col_segment, col_annot, params$ann_of_interest))
required_meta_cols <- required_meta_cols[!is.na(required_meta_cols) & nzchar(required_meta_cols)]
missing_required_meta_cols <- setdiff(required_meta_cols, colnames(target_pheno))
# Fail early with explicit missing required columns to avoid opaque ggplot/data errors later.
if (length(missing_required_meta_cols) > 0) {
  stop(
    "Missing required metadata columns in pData(target_spatial_data): ",
    paste(missing_required_meta_cols, collapse = ", "),
    ". Update params$meta_cols and params$ann_of_interest before running normalisation."
  )
}

slide_metadata_available <- metadata_available(col_slide, target_pheno)
region_metadata_available <- metadata_available(col_region, target_pheno)
metadata_source_label <- function(col_name) {
  if (is.null(col_name) || length(col_name) != 1 || is.na(col_name) || !nzchar(col_name)) {
    return("not configured")
  }
  col_name
}

plot_slide_col <- ".normalisation_slide_group"
plot_region_col <- ".normalisation_disease_group"
plot_segment_col <- ".normalisation_segment_group"
plot_annotation_col <- ".normalisation_annotation_label"

normalisation_plot_metadata <- target_pheno
normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_slide_col]] <- if (slide_metadata_available) {
  as.character(target_pheno[[col_slide]])
} else {
  rep("Slide metadata unavailable", nrow(target_pheno))
}
normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_region_col]] <- if (region_metadata_available) {
  as.character(target_pheno[[col_region]])
} else {
  rep("Disease-status metadata unavailable", nrow(target_pheno))
}
normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_segment_col]] <- as.character(target_pheno[[col_segment]])
normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_annotation_col]] <- as.character(target_pheno[[col_annot]])

normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_slide_col]][is.na(normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_slide_col]]) | normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_slide_col]] == ""] <- "Slide metadata unavailable"
normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_region_col]][is.na(normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_region_col]]) | normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_region_col]] == ""] <- "Disease-status metadata unavailable"
normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_segment_col]][is.na(normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_segment_col]]) | normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_segment_col]] == ""] <- "Segment metadata unavailable"
normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_annotation_col]][is.na(normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_annotation_col]]) | normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_annotation_col]] == ""] <- "Annotation metadata unavailable"

slide_legend_label <- if (slide_metadata_available) {
  glue::glue("Slide ({col_slide})")
} else {
  "Slide (not available)"
}
region_legend_label <- if (region_metadata_available) {
  glue::glue("Disease status ({col_region})")
} else {
  "Disease status (not available)"
}

normalisation_plot_grouping_summary <- tibble::tibble(
  plot_role = c("Boxplot fill", "Boxplot facet", "Density color", "Density linetype", "Density facet"),
  metadata_source = c(
    metadata_source_label(col_slide),
    metadata_source_label(col_region),
    metadata_source_label(col_slide),
    metadata_source_label(col_region),
    metadata_source_label(col_segment)
  ),
  status = c(
    ifelse(slide_metadata_available, "used", "not found; fallback group used"),
    ifelse(region_metadata_available, "used", "not found; fallback group used"),
    ifelse(slide_metadata_available, "used", "not found; fallback group used"),
    ifelse(region_metadata_available, "used", "not found; fallback group used"),
    "used"
  ),
  plot_behavior = c(
    ifelse(slide_metadata_available, "Samples are filled by slide.", "Samples use a single slide-unavailable group."),
    ifelse(region_metadata_available, "Samples are faceted by disease status.", "Samples use a single disease-status-unavailable facet."),
    ifelse(slide_metadata_available, "Density curves are colored by slide.", "Density curves use a single slide-unavailable color group."),
    ifelse(region_metadata_available, "Density curves are linetyped by disease status.", "Density curves use a single disease-status-unavailable linetype group."),
    "Density plots are faceted by segment."
  )
)

knitr::kable(
  normalisation_plot_grouping_summary,
  caption = "Normalisation distribution plot grouping summary. Use this table to confirm which metadata columns drive boxplot and density encodings."
)
Normalisation distribution plot grouping summary. Use this table to confirm which metadata columns drive boxplot and density encodings.
plot_role metadata_source status plot_behavior
Boxplot fill slide_name used Samples are filled by slide.
Boxplot facet disease_status used Samples are faceted by disease status.
Density color slide_name used Density curves are colored by slide.
Density linetype disease_status used Density curves are linetyped by disease status.
Density facet segment used Density plots are faceted by segment.
Show code 
if (!slide_metadata_available || !region_metadata_available) {
  missing_optional_groupings <- c(
    if (!slide_metadata_available) glue::glue("slide metadata column `{metadata_source_label(col_slide)}`") else NULL,
    if (!region_metadata_available) glue::glue("disease-status metadata column `{metadata_source_label(col_region)}`") else NULL
  )
  message(
    "Optional normalisation plot grouping metadata not found: ",
    paste(missing_optional_groupings, collapse = "; "),
    ". Distribution plots will continue with explicit fallback groups."
  )
}

# Optional disease-label cleanup to avoid separate categories caused by spelling or case differences.
harmonise_disease <- function(x) {
  x_chr <- trimws(as.character(x))
  x_low <- tolower(x_chr)
  dplyr::case_when(
    x_low %in% c("tumour", "tumor") ~ "tumour",
    x_low %in% c("healthy", "normal", "control", "non-tumour", "non-tumor") ~ "healthy",
    is.na(x_chr) | x_chr == "" ~ NA_character_,
    TRUE ~ x_chr
  )
}

if (isTRUE(params$harmonise_disease_labels) && region_metadata_available) {
  pData(target_spatial_data)[, col_region] <- harmonise_disease(pData(target_spatial_data)[, col_region])
  normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_region_col]] <- harmonise_disease(normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_region_col]])
}

disease_counts <- table(normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_region_col]], useNA = "ifany")
message("Disease-state counts in normalisation input:")
disease_count_table <- tibble::enframe(disease_counts, name = "disease_group", value = "sample_count")
knitr::kable(
  disease_count_table,
  caption = "Disease-group sample counts used by normalisation distribution plots."
)
Disease-group sample counts used by normalisation distribution plots.
disease_group sample_count
healthy 111
tumour 142
Show code 
if (region_metadata_available && sum(disease_counts > 0, na.rm = TRUE) < 2) {
  knitr::asis_output(glue::glue(
    "\n::: {{.callout-warning}}\n",
    "## Disease-state review\n",
    "Only one disease state is present in the normalisation input for `{col_region}`. ",
    "Check the filtering outputs from earlier stages, including GeoDiff segment filtering, before interpreting group-faceted plots.\n",
    ":::\n"
  ))
} else if (!region_metadata_available) {
  knitr::asis_output(glue::glue(
    "\n::: {{.callout-warning}}\n",
    "## Disease-state review\n",
    "The configured disease-status column `{metadata_source_label(col_region)}` was not found. ",
    "Normalisation distribution plots will render with a single fallback disease-status group. ",
    "Update `params$meta_cols$value$region` if disease-level comparison is required.\n",
    ":::\n"
  ))
}

Plot negative control probe summaries

Generate signal-to-noise context plots comparing Q3 and negative-probe summaries by annotation groups.

Show code 
# Graph Q3 value vs negGeoMean of Negatives
ann_of_interest <- params$ann_of_interest

#get the negative probes
negativeProbefData <- subset(fData(target_spatial_data), CodeClass == "Negative")
neg_probes <- unique(negativeProbefData$TargetName)

Stat_data <-
  data.frame(
    row.names  = colnames(exprs(target_spatial_data)),
    Segment    = colnames(exprs(target_spatial_data)),
    Annotation = pData(target_spatial_data)[, ann_of_interest],
    Q3         = unlist(apply(exprs(target_spatial_data), 2, quantile, params$q3_quantile, na.rm = TRUE)),
    # Per-segment negative-probe geometric mean. drop = FALSE keeps this a matrix
    # when several negative targets exist (otherwise the column is mislabeled raw
    # counts, or breaks when neg_probes has length > 1).
    NegProbe   = apply(
      exprs(target_spatial_data)[neg_probes, , drop = FALSE], 2,
      function(x) exp(mean(log(x[x > 0])))
    )
  )


Stat_data_m <- gather(Stat_data, key = "Statistic", value = "Value", c("Q3", "NegProbe"))

plt1 <- ggplot(Stat_data_m, aes(x = .data$Value, fill = .data$Statistic)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40) + protocol_bw_theme() +
  scale_x_continuous(trans = "log2") +
  facet_wrap(~Annotation, nrow = 1) +
  # Okabe-Ito accessible colours for the two distribution statistics.
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Q3"       = unname(protocol_okabe_ito()[["blue"]]),
    "NegProbe" = unname(protocol_okabe_ito()[["orange"]])
  )) +
  labs(x = "Counts", y = "Segments, #")

plt2 <- ggplot(Stat_data, aes(x = .data$NegProbe, y = .data$Q3, color = .data$Annotation)) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, lty = "dashed", color = "darkgray") +
  geom_point() + guides(color = "none") + protocol_bw_theme() +
  scale_x_continuous(trans = "log2") +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log2") +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  labs(x = "Negative Probe GeoMean, Counts", y = "Q3 Value, Counts")

plt3 <- ggplot(Stat_data, aes(x = .data$NegProbe, y = .data$Q3 / .data$NegProbe, color = .data$Annotation)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, lty = "dashed", color = "darkgray") +
  geom_point() + protocol_bw_theme() +
  scale_x_continuous(trans = "log2") +
  scale_y_continuous(trans = "log2") +
  theme(aspect.ratio = 1) +
  labs(x = "Negative Probe GeoMean, Counts", y = "Q3/NegProbe Value, Counts")

# Bottom row: place the two scatter plots side by side with an empty spacer
# column between them so their axis titles no longer overlap, and give the second
# scatter its own "C" label (it was previously unlabelled, so the figure appeared
# to contain only panels A and B).
btm_row <- plot_grid(
  plt2, NULL, plt3,
  nrow = 1,
  labels = c("B", "", "C"),
  rel_widths = c(0.42, 0.1, 0.48)
)
q3vsneg <- plot_grid(plt1, btm_row, ncol = 1, labels = c("A", ""), rel_heights = c(0.48, 0.52))


# Save this figure only when persistence is enabled in YAML.
if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
  save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "q3vsneg.png"), plot = q3vsneg,
            base_height = 8, base_width = 10, bg = "white")
}



q3vsneg

Explore normalisation methods

Perform TMM normalisation

Construct two edgeR objects and compute TMM normalisation factors for later use.

  • tmm_dge_with_metadata: full DGEList with counts, sample metadata, and feature annotations (used for most later summaries/plots).
  • tmm_dge_counts_only: minimal DGEList containing counts plus TMM factors only. This object is not read by later notebooks in the current pipeline; it is retained for audit checks.
Show code 
if (isTRUE(params$load_existing_norm_objects) && file_exists(tmm_rds_path)) {
  tmm_dge_with_metadata <- readRDS(tmm_rds_path)
  if (file_exists(tmm_minimal_counts_rds_path)) {
    tmm_dge_counts_only <- readRDS(tmm_minimal_counts_rds_path)
  } else {
    tmm_dge_counts_only <- DGEList(counts = exprs(target_spatial_data))
    tmm_dge_counts_only <- calcNormFactors(tmm_dge_counts_only, method = "TMM")
  }
  message(glue::glue("Loaded full TMM DGEList (with metadata): {tmm_rds_path}"))
  message(glue::glue("Loaded/constructed minimal TMM DGEList (counts only): {tmm_minimal_counts_rds_path}"))
} else {
  # Align phenotype rows with expression matrix columns before DGEList creation.
  pheno <- pData(target_spatial_data)[row.names(pData(target_spatial_data)) %in% colnames(exprs(target_spatial_data)),]

  # Align feature annotation rows with expression matrix rows.
  genes <- fData(target_spatial_data)[row.names(fData(target_spatial_data)) %in% rownames(exprs(target_spatial_data)),]

  tmm_dge_with_metadata <- DGEList(counts = exprs(target_spatial_data), lib.size = colSums(exprs(target_spatial_data)),
          samples = pheno,
          genes = genes)

  # `calcNormFactors(..., method='TMM')` estimates composition-bias correction factors
  # used by edgeR CPM calculations for between-sample comparability.
  tmm_dge_with_metadata <- calcNormFactors(tmm_dge_with_metadata, method = "TMM")

  tmm_dge_counts_only <- DGEList(counts = exprs(target_spatial_data))
  # Secondary minimal DGEList version retained for compatibility checks.
  tmm_dge_counts_only <- calcNormFactors(tmm_dge_counts_only, method = "TMM")

  # Persist normalised objects only when save_rds toggle is enabled.
  if (isTRUE(params$save_rds)) {
    # TMM_normalised_spatial_data.RDS: full metadata-rich TMM DGEList used by later notebooks.
    saveRDS(tmm_dge_with_metadata,  file = tmm_rds_path)
    # TMM_normalised_spatial_minimal_Counts_data.RDS: minimal counts-only TMM DGEList retained for lightweight audit use.
    saveRDS(tmm_dge_counts_only, file = tmm_minimal_counts_rds_path)
  }
}

# This alias supports later code sections that still use the older object name.
TMM_norm_data <- tmm_dge_with_metadata
TMM output files

Use TMM_normalised_spatial_data.RDS for later analysis because it preserves the raw count matrix, TMM normalization factors, sample metadata, and feature annotations required by later stages. TMM_normalised_spatial_minimal_Counts_data.RDS is a compact counts-only TMM object retained for audit checks; later notebooks in the current pipeline do not read it.

TMM normalisation output summary. Inspect the metadata-rich object before later analysis; use the minimal counts-only object only for audit checks.
output_file object_created contents pipeline_role
TMM_normalised_spatial_data.RDS tmm_dge_with_metadata Full metadata-rich TMM DGEList with raw counts, TMM scaling factors, sample metadata, and feature annotations. Primary TMM output used by this notebook and later stages.
TMM_normalised_spatial_minimal_Counts_data.RDS tmm_dge_counts_only Minimal counts-only TMM DGEList with TMM scaling factors. Optional audit file; not read by later notebooks in the current pipeline.

Perform Q3 normalisation

Apply upper-quantile normalisation at the configured quantile to produce a Q3-normalised assay.

Show code 
if (isTRUE(params$load_existing_norm_objects) && file_exists(normalised_rds_path)) {
  target_spatial_data <- readRDS(normalised_rds_path)
  message(glue::glue("Loaded existing normalised GeoMx object from: {normalised_rds_path}"))
} else {
  # Q3 norm (75th percentile) for WTA/CTA  with or without custom spike-ins
  # `NanoStringNCTools::normalize` with `norm_method = "quant"` applies upper-quantile
  # scaling and writes the result into `toElt` while preserving raw counts in `exprs`.
  target_spatial_data <- NanoStringNCTools::normalize(target_spatial_data ,
                               norm_method = "quant", 
                               desiredQuantile = params$q3_quantile,
                               toElt = params$q3_toElt)
}

if (!(params$q3_toElt %in% assayDataElementNames(target_spatial_data))) {
  stop("Q3 assay '", params$q3_toElt, "' was not found in target_spatial_data.")
}

Perform negative probe normalisation

Apply negative-probe normalisation and save the combined normalised GeoMx object.

Show code 
if (!(isTRUE(params$load_existing_norm_objects) && file_exists(normalised_rds_path))) {
  # neg
  # `NanoStringNCTools::normalize` with `norm_method = "neg"` scales expression by
  # negative-control probes to reduce background-driven technical variation.
  target_spatial_data <- NanoStringNCTools::normalize(target_spatial_data ,
                               norm_method = "neg",
                               toElt = params$neg_toElt
  )

  # This RDS file contains both the Q3 and negative normalisation.
  # Persist normalised objects only when save_rds toggle is enabled.
  if (isTRUE(params$save_rds)) {
    saveRDS(target_spatial_data, file = normalised_rds_path)
  }
}

if (!(params$neg_toElt %in% assayDataElementNames(target_spatial_data))) {
  stop("Negative-probe assay '", params$neg_toElt, "' was not found in target_spatial_data.")
}

Perform GeoDiff-normalized normmat calculation

Run Poisson-threshold normalisation using the GeoDiff-filtered count object and configured high-quality segment/feature inputs.

Show code 
if (isTRUE(params$load_existing_norm_objects) && file_exists(geodiff_norm_rds_path)) {
  spatial_data_geoDiff <- readRDS(geodiff_norm_rds_path)
  ROIs_high <- sampleNames(spatial_data_geoDiff)
  geodiff_route_active <- TRUE
  message(glue::glue("Loaded existing GeoDiff-normalized object from: {geodiff_norm_rds_path}"))
} else if (!geodiff_input_exists && geodiff_norm_exists) {
  spatial_data_geoDiff <- readRDS(geodiff_norm_rds_path)
  ROIs_high <- sampleNames(spatial_data_geoDiff)
  geodiff_route_active <- TRUE
  message(glue::glue(
    "Loaded existing GeoDiff-normalized object from {geodiff_norm_rds_path} because no GeoDiff-filtered count object was available."
  ))
} else if (geodiff_input_exists) {
  if (!requireNamespace("GeoDiff", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop(
      "GeoDiff optional route is available because a GeoDiff-filtered count object exists, but the GeoDiff package is not installed. ",
      "Install GeoDiff or remove the GeoDiff-filtered count object file if you intend to run only Q3/TMM normalisation.",
      call. = FALSE
    )
  }

  if (is.null(spatial_data_geoDiff)) {
    spatial_data_geoDiff <- readRDS(geodiff_path)
  }

  posdat <- spatial_data_geoDiff[-which(fData(spatial_data_geoDiff)$CodeClass == "Negative"), ]
  features_all <- rownames(posdat)
  # GeoDiff high-signal feature mask is used to subset matrix before plotting/comparison.
  features_high <- rownames(fData(spatial_data_geoDiff))[fData(spatial_data_geoDiff)$feature_high_fitNBth == 1]

  ROIs_high <- sampleNames(spatial_data_geoDiff)

  spatial_data_geoDiff <- GeoDiff::fitPoisthNorm(object = spatial_data_geoDiff,
                          ROIs_high = ROIs_high,
                          features_high=features_high,features_all=features_all,
                          threshold_mean = notes(spatial_data_geoDiff)[["bgMean"]],
                          sizescalebythreshold = TRUE)

  geodiff_route_active <- TRUE

  # Only save high quality segments
  # Persist normalised objects only when save_rds toggle is enabled.
  if (isTRUE(params$save_rds)) {
    saveRDS(spatial_data_geoDiff[, ROIs_high], file = geodiff_norm_rds_path)
  }
} else {
  message(geodiff_skip_message)
}

if (isTRUE(geodiff_route_active) && !("normmat" %in% assayDataElementNames(spatial_data_geoDiff))) {
  stop("GeoDiff assay 'normmat' was not found in spatial_data_geoDiff.")
}

Compare normalisation outputs

Boxplots and density plots of normalised data

This section produces a consistent set of visual summaries (boxplots and densities) across normalisation methods to support side-by-side technical comparison.

For each method, both boxplots and density plots now show slide and disease-status context:

  • boxplots: fill by slide and facet by disease status,
  • densities: color by slide and linetype by disease status.

Sample labels are keyed by sample name so they remain correct when disease-status facets use independent x-axis scales.

Show code 
normalisation_sample_labels <- stats::setNames(
  janitor::make_clean_names(
    normalisation_plot_metadata[[plot_annotation_col]],
    case = "parsed"
  ),
  rownames(normalisation_plot_metadata)
)

normalisation_sample_axis_labels <- function(sample_ids) {
  resolved_labels <- unname(normalisation_sample_labels[sample_ids])
  unresolved <- is.na(resolved_labels) | resolved_labels == ""
  resolved_labels[unresolved] <- sample_ids[unresolved]
  resolved_labels
}
  • Raw data
  • TMM normalised
  • Q3 normalised
  • Negative probe normalisation
  • GeoDiff normalized

The plots below provide a baseline view of unnormalised count distributions.

Show code 
rawData <- log2(exprs(target_spatial_data)) %>%  as.data.frame() %>%
  gather() 


metaData <- normalisation_plot_metadata



rawDataPlot <- merge(rawData, metaData, by.x = "key", by.y = "row.names") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = .data$key, y = .data$value, fill = .data[[plot_slide_col]])) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ylab("log2 raw counts") + xlab("Segment") +
  labs(fill = slide_legend_label) +
  protocol_cowplot_theme() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = normalisation_sample_axis_labels) +
  facet_wrap(vars(!!rlang::sym(plot_region_col)), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(axis.text.x = normalisation_boxplot_axis_text)


# Save this figure only when persistence is enabled in YAML.
if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
  save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "rawDataBoxplots.png"), plot = rawDataPlot,
            base_height = 8, base_width = 8, bg = "white")
}


print(rawDataPlot)
Show code 
rawDataDensity <- rawData %>%
  merge(., metaData, by.x = "key", by.y = "row.names") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = .data$value, color = .data[[plot_slide_col]], linetype = .data[[plot_region_col]])) +
  geom_density() +
  ylab("log2 raw counts") + xlab("Segment") +
  labs(color = slide_legend_label, linetype = region_legend_label) +
  protocol_cowplot_theme() +
  theme(axis.text.x = protocol_rotated_axis_text) +
  theme(legend.position = "right") +
  facet_wrap(vars(!!rlang::sym(plot_segment_col)))

# Save this figure only when persistence is enabled in YAML.
if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
  save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "rawDataDensity.png"), plot = rawDataDensity,
            base_height = 8, base_width = 8, bg = "white")
}


print(rawDataDensity)
Show code 
TMMNormalised <- cpm(TMM_norm_data, log=TRUE) %>%  as.data.frame() %>%
  gather() 


metaData <- normalisation_plot_metadata


TMMNormalisedPlot <- merge(TMMNormalised, metaData, by.x = "key", by.y = "row.names") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = .data$key, y = .data$value, fill = .data[[plot_slide_col]])) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  ylab("log2 TMM counts") + xlab("Segment") +
  labs(fill = slide_legend_label) +
  protocol_cowplot_theme() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = normalisation_sample_axis_labels) +
  facet_wrap(vars(!!rlang::sym(plot_region_col)), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(axis.text.x = normalisation_boxplot_axis_text)


# Save this figure only when persistence is enabled in YAML.
if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
  save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "TMMNormalisedPlot.png"), plot = TMMNormalisedPlot,
            base_height = 8, base_width = 8, bg = "white")
}

Show code

  merge(., metaData, by.x = "key", by.y = "row.names") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = .data$value, color = .data[[plot_slide_col]], linetype = .data[[plot_region_col]])) +
  geom_density() +
  ylab("log2 TMM counts") + xlab("Segment") +
  labs(color = slide_legend_label, linetype = region_legend_label) +
  protocol_cowplot_theme() +
  theme(axis.text.x = protocol_rotated_axis_text) +
  theme(legend.position = "right") +
  facet_wrap(vars(!!rlang::sym(plot_segment_col)))

# Save this figure only when persistence is enabled in YAML.
if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
  save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "TMMNormalisedDensity.png"), plot = TMMNormalisedDensity,
            base_height = 8, base_width = 8, bg = "white")

This panel displays the Q3-normalised distributions using the same plotting structure as other methods.

Show code 
q3Normalised <- log2(assayDataElement(target_spatial_data, elt = params$q3_toElt)) %>%  as.data.frame() %>%
  gather()

This panel displays GeoDiff-normalized normmat distributions for the same metadata groupings.

Show code 
if (isTRUE(geodiff_route_active)) {
  geoDiffNormalised <- assayDataElement(spatial_data_geoDiff[, ROIs_high], elt = "normmat") %>%  as.data.frame() %>%  gather()

  metaData <- normalisation_plot_metadata


  geoDiffNormalisedPlot <- merge(geoDiffNormalised, metaData, by.x = "key", by.y = "row.names") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data$key, y = .data$value, fill = .data[[plot_slide_col]])) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ylab("log Poisson Threshold Normalized values") + xlab("Segment") +
    labs(fill = slide_legend_label) +
    protocol_cowplot_theme() +
    scale_x_discrete(labels = normalisation_sample_axis_labels) +
    facet_wrap(vars(!!rlang::sym(plot_region_col)), scales = "free_x") +
    theme(axis.text.x = normalisation_boxplot_axis_text)


  # Save this figure only when persistence is enabled in YAML.
  if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
    save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "geoDiffNormalisedPlot.png"), plot = geoDiffNormalisedPlot,
              base_height = 8, base_width = 8, bg = "white")
  }

  print(geoDiffNormalisedPlot)
} else {
  message(glue::glue(
    "GeoDiff boxplot skipped: no GeoDiff-normalized object is available at {geodiff_norm_rds_path}. ",
    "Q3/TMM boxplots above remain available for non-GeoDiff review."
  ))
}
Show code 
if (isTRUE(geodiff_route_active)) {


  geoDiffNormalisedDensity <- geoDiffNormalised %>%
    merge(., metaData, by.x = "key", by.y = "row.names") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data$value, color = .data[[plot_slide_col]], linetype = .data[[plot_region_col]])) +
    geom_density() +
    ylab("log Poisson Threshold Normalized values") + xlab("Segment") +
    labs(color = slide_legend_label, linetype = region_legend_label) +
    protocol_cowplot_theme() +
    theme(axis.text.x = protocol_rotated_axis_text) +
    theme(legend.position = "right") +
    facet_wrap(vars(!!rlang::sym(plot_segment_col)))

  # Save this figure only when persistence is enabled in YAML.
  if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
    save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "geoDiffNormalisedDensity.png"), plot = geoDiffNormalisedDensity,
              base_height = 8, base_width = 8, bg = "white")
  }


  print(geoDiffNormalisedDensity)

} else {
  message(glue::glue(
    "GeoDiff density plot skipped: no GeoDiff-normalized object is available at {geodiff_norm_rds_path}. ",
    "Q3/TMM density plots above remain available for non-GeoDiff review."
  ))
}

Segment marker expression

Plot configured marker genes across segment groups for each normalization output as an additional QC/context view. Marker availability is checked independently for every method before plotting.

Why marker availability can differ between methods

TMM, Q3, and negative-probe normalization use the Stage 1 detection-filtered feature set. The optional GeoDiff route starts from the earlier Stage 1 QC-pass object and applies its own feature modeling, so a requested marker can be present for GeoDiff but absent from the other normalization matrices. An unavailable marker is reported and skipped; it is not evidence that normalization removed the marker.

Show code 
# Normalize and validate the user-supplied YAML marker list once.
requested_markers <- as.character(unlist(params$marker_genes, use.names = FALSE))
requested_markers <- trimws(requested_markers)
requested_markers <- requested_markers[!is.na(requested_markers) & nzchar(requested_markers)]
requested_markers <- requested_markers[!duplicated(toupper(requested_markers))]

if (length(requested_markers) == 0) {
  stop(
    "params$marker_genes is empty. Provide at least one marker gene before rendering Stage 3.",
    call. = FALSE
  )
}

# Build each method matrix once so validation and plotting use the same feature universe.
marker_matrices <- list(
  TMM = cpm(TMM_norm_data, log = TRUE),
  Q3 = log2(assayDataElement(target_spatial_data, elt = params$q3_toElt)),
  `Negative probe` = log2(assayDataElement(target_spatial_data, elt = params$neg_toElt))
)

if (isTRUE(geodiff_route_active)) {
  marker_matrices[["GeoDiff"]] <- assayDataElement(
    spatial_data_geoDiff[, ROIs_high],
    elt = "normmat"
  )
}

match_markers_in_matrix <- function(mat, method_name, requested) {
  matrix_features <- rownames(mat)
  if (is.null(matrix_features)) matrix_features <- character()

  matched_features <- matrix_features[
    match(toupper(requested), toupper(matrix_features))
  ]

  tibble::tibble(
    Normalization_method = method_name,
    Requested_marker = requested,
    Matched_feature = matched_features,
    Available = !is.na(matched_features)
  )
}

marker_lookups <- purrr::imap(
  marker_matrices,
  ~ match_markers_in_matrix(.x, .y, requested_markers)
)

marker_availability <- dplyr::bind_rows(marker_lookups) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Status = dplyr::if_else(.data$Available, "Available", "Not present; plot facet skipped"),
    Matched_feature = dplyr::coalesce(.data$Matched_feature, "—")
  )

marker_availability_table <- marker_availability %>%
  dplyr::transmute(
    `Normalization method` = .data$Normalization_method,
    `Requested marker` = .data$Requested_marker,
    `Matched matrix feature` = .data$Matched_feature,
    Status = .data$Status
  )

knitr::kable(
  marker_availability_table,
  row.names = FALSE,
  caption = paste(
    "Marker-gene availability by normalization method.",
    "Review unavailable markers before interpreting differences between method-specific panels."
  )
)
Marker-gene availability by normalization method. Review unavailable markers before interpreting differences between method-specific panels.
Normalization method Requested marker Matched matrix feature Status
TMM PTPRC — Not present; plot facet skipped
TMM KRT8 KRT8 Available
Q3 PTPRC — Not present; plot facet skipped
Q3 KRT8 KRT8 Available
Negative probe PTPRC — Not present; plot facet skipped
Negative probe KRT8 KRT8 Available
GeoDiff PTPRC PTPRC Available
GeoDiff KRT8 KRT8 Available
Show code 
missing_marker_summary <- marker_availability %>%
  dplyr::filter(!.data$Available) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(.data$Normalization_method) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    Missing_markers = paste(.data$Requested_marker, collapse = ", "),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

if (nrow(missing_marker_summary) > 0) {
  warning_lines <- paste0(
    "- **", missing_marker_summary$Normalization_method, "**: ",
    missing_marker_summary$Missing_markers
  )

  knitr::asis_output(paste0(
    "\n::: {.callout-warning}\n",
    "## Requested markers unavailable in one or more matrices\n",
    "The following markers are not present in the indicated method-specific feature sets and will be skipped:\n\n",
    paste(warning_lines, collapse = "\n"),
    "\n\nThe notebook will continue. Confirm that the YAML marker list and earlier-stage feature-filtering decisions are appropriate for the study.\n",
    ":::\n"
  ))
}
Requested markers unavailable in one or more matrices

The following markers are not present in the indicated method-specific feature sets and will be skipped:

  • Negative probe: PTPRC
  • Q3: PTPRC
  • TMM: PTPRC

The notebook will continue. Confirm that the YAML marker list and earlier-stage feature-filtering decisions are appropriate for the study.

Show code 
marker_plot_from_mat <- function(mat, metaData, col_segment, marker_lookup) {
  available_markers <- marker_lookup %>%
    dplyr::filter(.data$Available)

  if (nrow(available_markers) == 0) return(NULL)

  marker_long <- purrr::map2_dfr(
    available_markers$Requested_marker,
    available_markers$Matched_feature,
    function(gene_nm, row_nm) {
      tibble::tibble(
        AOI = colnames(mat),
        Expression = as.numeric(mat[row_nm, , drop = TRUE]),
        Gene = gene_nm
      )
    }
  ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      Gene = factor(.data$Gene, levels = available_markers$Requested_marker)
    )

  marker_metadata <- metaData %>%
    as.data.frame() %>%
    tibble::rownames_to_column(var = "AOI")

  marker_long %>%
    dplyr::inner_join(marker_metadata, by = "AOI") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = .data[[col_segment]], y = .data$Expression, fill = .data[[col_segment]])) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    ylab("log2 normalized values") +
    xlab("Segment") +
    protocol_cowplot_theme() +
    theme(axis.text.x = protocol_rotated_axis_text) +
    facet_wrap(~Gene, scales = "free_y")
}

marker_plot_skip_note <- function(method_name) {
  knitr::asis_output(paste0(
    "\n::: {.callout-warning}\n",
    "## ", method_name, " marker plot skipped\n",
    "None of the requested marker genes are present in the ", method_name,
    " matrix. Review the availability table above and update `params$marker_genes` if another marker is required.\n",
    ":::\n"
  ))
}

metaData <- normalisation_plot_metadata
  • TMM
  • Q3
  • Negative probe normalization
  • GeoDiff
Show code 
g <- marker_plot_from_mat(
  marker_matrices[["TMM"]],
  metaData,
  col_segment,
  marker_lookups[["TMM"]]
)

if (is.null(g)) {
  marker_plot_skip_note("TMM")
} else {
  # Save output conditionally to support fast exploratory runs without file writes.
  if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
    save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "antibodyValues_TMM.png"), plot = g, bg = "white")
  }
  print(g)
}
Show code 
g <- marker_plot_from_mat(
  marker_matrices[["Q3"]],
  metaData,
  col_segment,
  marker_lookups[["Q3"]]
)

if (is.null(g)) {
  marker_plot_skip_note("Q3")
} else {
  # Save output conditionally to support fast exploratory runs without file writes.
  if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
    save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "antibodyValues_Q3.png"), plot = g, bg = "white")
  }
  print(g)
}
Show code 
g <- marker_plot_from_mat(
  marker_matrices[["Negative probe"]],
  metaData,
  col_segment,
  marker_lookups[["Negative probe"]]
)

if (is.null(g)) {
  marker_plot_skip_note("Negative probe")
} else {
  # Save output conditionally to support fast exploratory runs without file writes.
  if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
    save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "antibodyValues_negProbe.png"), plot = g, bg = "white")
  }
  print(g)
}
Show code 
if (isTRUE(geodiff_route_active)) {
  g <- marker_plot_from_mat(
    marker_matrices[["GeoDiff"]],
    metaData,
    col_segment,
    marker_lookups[["GeoDiff"]]
  )

  if (is.null(g)) {
    marker_plot_skip_note("GeoDiff")
  } else {
    # Save output conditionally to support fast exploratory runs without file writes.
    if (isTRUE(params$save_plots)) {
      save_plot(filename = path(out_fig_norm, "antibodyValues_GeoDiff.png"), plot = g, bg = "white")
    }
    print(g)
  }
} else {
  message(glue::glue(
    "GeoDiff marker-gene plot skipped: no GeoDiff-normalized object is available at {geodiff_norm_rds_path}. ",
    "Non-GeoDiff marker plots remain available above."
  ))
}

Use the generated plots and saved outputs as inputs to later model-selection and differential-expression workflows.
